125101.explanation: In recent years, Utrecht has used property tax to fill budget gaps. Homeowners are overburdened. It is time to freeze the property tax.

125102.explanation: The revenue from dog tax goes into general funds and is disproportionate to the low public costs of keeping dogs. However, it is important to consider the environmental pollution that dog ownership can cause.

125105.explanation: It is the municipality’s duty to help all residents who are homeless or at risk of homelessness with housing. This applies to people who are divorcing, as well as young people or asylum seekers with residence permits.

125110.explanation: It is important that the mix of social rental, affordable rental, affordable owner-occupied, and expensive rental or owner-occupied homes is balanced per neighborhood.

125115.explanation: Where possible, residents should be able to park in front of their door. Where public space benefits from more space for people and nature, and where canal basements cannot withstand car and truck traffic, parking spaces may be reduced. However, parking must never be made completely impossible.

125116.explanation: Stadsbelang Utrecht supports traffic around the city, not through it. Of course, this must not come at the expense of accessible neighborhoods.

125117.explanation: Sex work is work. It is important that sex workers have good and safe workplaces or can work from home with a permit. The municipality should allow them to work freely where possible and appropriate without being coercive. Dialogue with other residents is also important.

125118.explanation: A housing corporation must be able to renew its portfolio and use part of the sales proceeds to realize this. However, sufficient social housing stock must remain available.

125119.explanation: It is important to invest in sustainable and creative solutions in consultation with residents.

125120.explanation: Safety is important, also in using e-bikes. The safety of road users benefits from separating slow and fast traffic (> 30 km/h) on bike paths. Each situation should be examined to determine what separation can be applied.

125122.explanation: The environmental zone has not been proven effective in achieving the goal of reducing fine dust and CO2.

125059.explanation: The PVV supports direct influence on governance.

125062.explanation: But appropriate measures should be taken in case of nuisance for local residents.

125063.explanation: The PVV is against the expansion of asylum seeker centers (AZCs).

125064.explanation: The PVV is against supporting illegal activities.

125070.explanation: We want existing standards to be properly enforced.

125073.explanation: Local broadcasting has an informative function...

125075.explanation: Subsidies only for families with a low income.

125077.explanation: We are in favor of protecting cultural heritage.

125079.explanation: Only in places where many incidents occur.

125081.explanation: This gives the BOAs the opportunity to better defend themselves against aggressive behavior.

125082.explanation: This measure offers opportunities for starters!

125088.explanation: Only for those who are capable of doing so.

125090.explanation: The PVV wants to combat poverty in Utrecht!

125094.explanation: Only in areas where safety is at stake.

125098.explanation: There is a shortage of healthcare personnel, so not feasible.

125100.explanation: Not a primary task of the municipality.

125101.explanation: The PVV prefers to see this lowered.

125102.explanation: Is a measure to fill the municipal treasury and is unnecessary bureaucracy.

125105.explanation: Is discrimination against our own residents.

125110.explanation: A nice ambition but not achievable.

125115.explanation: The PVV is against the bullying of motorists.

125117.explanation: The PVV believes this will limit abuses and illegal practices.

125118.explanation: However, the number of social rental homes must remain at an adequate level.

125119.explanation: On the initiative of the residents themselves.

125120.explanation: Not conducive to flow and safety on the roadway.

125122.explanation: A measure to annoy entrepreneurs and motorists.

125059.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes Utrechters should be able to influence major and impactful decisions. Through referenda, residents gain real influence, more support is created, and policies become better and fairer. For smaller matters, UtrechtNu! wants more support measurements among directly involved residents.

125062.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes the hospitality sector should have space to set their own opening hours. With attention to local residents: the municipality intervenes proportionally in case of nuisance. Freedom where possible, limits where necessary.

125063.explanation: Utrecht already contributes a lot to asylum reception. The pressure on housing, care, and safety is high and the limit has been reached. Therefore, we believe that reception in Utrecht should be scaled down and not further expanded. The national government must take responsibility and pay.

125064.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes rejected asylum seekers should not be structurally housed by the municipality. Only very basic bed, bath, and bread shelter is acceptable, to prevent people wandering the streets as happened at the Bollendak. Fully funded by the national government and aimed at return.

125066.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted. Additional bans are patronizing and often symbolic policies. Intervention is only necessary in cases of demonstrable nuisance, not with general bans for everyone.

125070.explanation: UtrechtNu! wants to allow room for festivals and parties, but with attention to neighborhood support. Nuisance should be addressed specifically with tailored measures and enforcement, not with increasingly strict general rules.

125073.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives more than half a million euros per year in municipal support. Local independent media are important, but for UtrechtNu! this is sufficient. Additional funding is not a given.

125075.explanation: UtrechtNu! finds music education important, but a general subsidy for all children goes too far. For low-income families there is already support through the U-pass. That is more targeted and fairer than a scheme without distinction.

125077.explanation: Monument care is not a luxury, but a legal duty. Especially for national monuments the municipality cannot cut costs here. Heritage determines Utrecht’s character and must be carefully protected.

125078.explanation: UtrechtNu! wants more visible enforcement officers in the neighborhoods. BOAs who know residents and act quickly on nuisance increase safety and trust. Safety is a basic condition for a livable city.

125079.explanation: UtrechtNu! sees camera surveillance as a useful tool to increase safety in places where problems persist. Used targeted, with clear goals and proper follow-up, and always with attention to proportionality and privacy.

125081.explanation: UtrechtNu! appreciates the work of enforcement officers (boa’s) and their commitment to safety in the neighborhoods. However, the police are responsible for using force and carrying weapons. Enforcement officers strengthen supervision, while the police enforce with means.

125082.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that first-time buyers could use a helping hand. A starter loan can help, but only within clear limits, such as the NHG (National Mortgage Guarantee). It is intended for those who need it.

125083.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that housing seekers from Utrecht should have priority for both social and mid-range rental housing. People who live here and have built their lives deserve a fair chance of finding a home in their own city.

125088.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that those receiving social assistance benefits and able to do so should give something back to society. That is fair and enhances self-worth. Always with customization, because not everyone can work.

125090.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that municipal taxes should, in principle, apply to everyone. Remission should not be automatic. Those who can contribute should do so, with room for customization in cases of genuine need.

125094.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that simply putting up a 30 km/h sign does not work and can sometimes even be less safe. Traffic safety requires logical street design. Without road adjustments, a lower speed limit is mostly false security.

125098.explanation: The municipality is not responsible for this.

125100.explanation: UtrechtNu! thinks it is important to address period poverty, but general free provision by the municipality goes too far.

125101.explanation: UtrechtNu! is strongly against an increase in the property tax. The property tax directly impacts residents and entrepreneurs and makes living and doing business more expensive. The municipality should critically review its own expenses, not continuously raise charges.

125102.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that the dog tax should be abolished. It is an outdated tax that says nothing about nuisance or responsibility. The municipality should manage costs more fairly and more targeted.

125105.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that social rental housing is primarily intended for housing seekers who already live here and are waiting. Priority for status holders increases pressure on the housing market and does not do justice to the long waiting times for Utrechters.

125110.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that a fixed standard of 45% social housing goes too far. The real problem is the shortage of mid-range rental housing, which prevents people from moving up. More balance in housing construction helps the entire housing market.

125115.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that the city center does not need to be completely car-free. A large part of the shopping area is already car-free. Further pushing for car-free zones achieves little and unnecessarily limits accessibility.

125116.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that the Catharijnesingel should remain open to through car and motorcycle traffic. The accessibility of the city center is already under pressure. More closures make the city unnecessarily less accessible.

125117.explanation: UtrechtNu! voted against this. The Paardenveld parking garage is an important source of income for the municipality and there is no support in District C. UtrechtNu! is not against sex work, but this location is unsuitable.

125118.explanation: UtrechtNu! believes that housing corporations should handle the sale of social rental homes carefully. Where possible, tenants should have the opportunity to buy their homes. This can contribute to moving up without unnecessarily depleting the social housing stock.

125119.explanation: UtrechtNu! values reuse and recycling, but the city already has recycling stations. Additional centers in every neighborhood are not realistic due to lack of space. Let’s make good use of and improve existing facilities.

125120.explanation: Antisocial fat bikers should be addressed for their behavior, not by a generic measure. UtrechtNu! believes that directing e-bikes and cargo bikes to the roadway is life-threatening and, for example, also forces parents with (small) children to cycle among car traffic.

125122.explanation: UtrechtNu! does not want to expand the environmental zone, but rather abolish it. It is symbolic policy that yields little and locks up the city. Residents, entrepreneurs, and professionals are affected while accessibility further declines.

125059.explanation: Important issues should have broad support. A referendum is the most democratic instrument.

125062.explanation: In the case of hospitality businesses, some regulation is necessary to also take surrounding residents into account.

125063.explanation: Closure is not necessary, but a stop on more is. First, the nuisance must be under control.

125064.explanation: No, because by accommodating rejected asylum seekers, more safe countries' residents keep coming. The only accommodation is at a deportation center.

125066.explanation: Yes, but by promoting awareness campaigns. However, enforcing behind the front door is considered a violation of privacy.

125070.explanation: Yes, in Utrecht the maximum number of decibels is absurdly high.

125073.explanation: The municipality already pays for the local broadcaster and also heavily subsidizes a door-to-door newspaper. The government wants to move away from this to safeguard the neutrality of the local broadcaster.

125075.explanation: Yes, music lessons are important for a child's development.

125077.explanation: Monuments tell our history and we should cherish them. Proper maintenance is part of that.

125078.explanation: Yes, there is currently a shortage and insecurity is increasing.

125079.explanation: Yes, this is urgently needed. Privacy no longer outweighs the protection of the citizen.

125081.explanation: No, we find this a too strong measure for an enforcement officer.

125082.explanation: Yes, especially with this overheated housing market, we as a municipality must do what we can to give starters a chance.

125088.explanation: May be removed. However, stimulating social contribution is important.

125090.explanation: Yes, because even lower incomes get into trouble.

125098.explanation: No, a caregiver is a caregiver and in that capacity must accept every person.

125100.explanation: That can be done via other organizations.

125101.explanation: That must be lowered. Now with another 10% added, it is no longer affordable.

125102.explanation: Yes, it is very strange that people have to pay tax for having a dog.

125105.explanation: That was a one-off due to backlog. In the meantime, little has been built, and corporations have been allowed to sell social rental homes. Now the burden must be fairly distributed.

125110.explanation: That sounds nice, but it often causes construction projects to stall. With 30% social housing and 70% more expensive homes, projects are profitable. Construction companies want to build and architects want to design, but people need to earn a living.

125115.explanation: The historic center must be a pedestrian zone: free of cars and bicycles.

125116.explanation: Absolutely not. It is the only connecting road between the neighborhoods. A trip of 5 minutes becomes 45 minutes. This isolates residents and shops.

125117.explanation: Absolutely not. It is the most unsuitable location for a sex work business.

125118.explanation: In the current housing shortage, with an increase in homeless people, every housing unit is needed.

125120.explanation: Difficult choice. On one hand, they often go too fast, and on the other hand, moving them to the roadway is the other extreme. Preferably not between the cars.

125122.explanation: Depends, it should not be a law. It can be allowed if the community supports it based on a support measurement.

125059.explanation: A reliable government ensures influence and involvement of residents in policies that affect them. Also, especially after the elections. JA21 supports citizen participation through a referendum to prevent fake consultation.

125062.explanation: A city that wants to be vibrant must dare to live at night. Therefore, we give horeca that complies with rules more leeway with longer opening hours in the inner city to offer opportunities to entrepreneurs, creatives, and students, keeping the inner city lively and safe.

125063.explanation: JA21 believes that the authority to open an asylum seeker center (azc) should always rest with the municipal council and should not be imposed from above. No more asylum reception should be added in the municipality, but existing locations cannot simply evict people onto the street or out of the city.

125064.explanation: We do not provide shelter to people who are illegally present in the Netherlands according to the law. Utrecht has too few shelter places and too many legal homeless people (over 2,000, including many youth) who have more right to a place in shelter.

125066.explanation: Everyone wants clean air and a healthy living environment, but we remain realistic: the city will not single-handedly save the world climate, but we can make Utrecht greener, cleaner, and more resilient with climate realism without costly symbolic projects or coercion. JA21 also supports reducing regulations.

125070.explanation: The standards are good now. Large festivals can be held in places where residents experience little to no nuisance. Even stricter rules would not be proportionate.

125073.explanation: Regional broadcasters are funded by the Ministry of Education, Culture and Science (OCW). An overall budget is set annually. JA21 believes the media, and thus local broadcasters, can function as independently as possible.

125075.explanation: Music lessons are already part of a child’s education program. We can only spend every euro once. JA21 chooses differently.

125077.explanation: Protecting cultural heritage is a matter of honor. Monuments tell the story of Utrecht. JA21 is proud of the historic inner city and history of Utrecht. We must protect and pass this on to the next generations. Our cultural heritage is too beautiful not to preserve.

125078.explanation: Safety in the neighborhoods and reducing crime is a high priority for JA21. Our first step is visible authority on the street, with investments in more and better enforcement.

125079.explanation: We do not accept intimidating behavior and crime in our streets. Our enforcement therefore focuses on addressing these problems. At locations with structural problems, we consider, in consultation with the police, the deployment of camera surveillance.

125081.explanation: Our first step is visible authority on the street, with investments in more police and enforcement in the neighborhoods, support for aid workers, expansion of city enforcement especially in the evenings and weekends with better equipment, powers under the municipal regulations (APV), and training.

125082.explanation: The municipality should focus on its statutory core tasks, such as safety, road maintenance, and youth care, and not spend taxpayers' money on aimless visions or projects without direct added value. The general reserve is the municipality's savings. The municipality is not a bank.

125083.explanation: We build for everyone, social rental housing for the lowest incomes, but especially affordable owner-occupied and mid-rent for average incomes with high percentages in projects. Maximum priority is given to Utrecht residents and crucial professions, no priority for status holders.

125088.explanation: JA21 believes too many people are sidelined and wants to motivate them to participate again. Doing socially meaningful work can contribute to this. By doing a few hours of meaningful work per week, the distance to the labor market decreases. Moreover, people come out of isolation.

125090.explanation: JA21 focuses more on helping people increase self-reliance than increasing dependence. The average Utrecht resident is increasingly burdened by rising costs. We focus on reducing these burdens to keep society strong.

125094.explanation: JA21 commits to well-maintained roads, bike and footpaths accessible to everyone. We prioritize tackling traffic unsafe locations. We ensure good traffic flow by not downgrading roads to 30 km/h unnecessarily. The fight against motorists must stop.

125098.explanation: The shortages in healthcare are too great to impose demands on care providers. JA21 believes care providers are competent for their tasks and act with integrity towards care recipients.

125100.explanation: Free tampons and pads do not exist. What is meant here is that the cost of tampons and pads is shifted to other citizens. A euro can only be spent once; JA21 chooses to improve youth care.

125101.explanation: JA21 believes homeownership should be encouraged rather than punished. A higher property valuation (WOZ) does not make the homeowner more financially capable; you cannot eat bricks. The property tax (ozb) in Utrecht is already relatively high. These costs must not rise further.

125102.explanation: A dog is actually a great joy for many lonely elderly people with a limited budget. People with dogs are less lonely, go outside and exercise more, and have more contact with others. Moreover, veterinary costs are often high.

125105.explanation: Status holders currently receive priority for social rental housing, which further pressures the already tight housing market and increases waiting lists for home seekers. JA21 wants to abolish this priority because it is unfair. Waiting times for Utrecht residents already reach about 12 years.

125110.explanation: The shortage of social rental housing is not solved by this demand, while the costs of social housing construction are enormous. JA21 prefers to focus more on other target groups (seniors, youth, mid-rent) to facilitate flow.

125115.explanation: JA21 has never supported the plan to have the plumber transfer his/her tools by cargo bike on the outskirts of the city. And mere transportation over the water of the canals and moats suffices for the beer boat, but not for supplying all shops.

125116.explanation: The Catharijnesingel was recently redesigned for a lot of money; closing it now is pointless waste of money and will create unnecessary extra traffic flows. Moreover, this further locks down the city. This affects the ordinary Utrecht resident.

125117.explanation: JA21 is not against sex workers but not at the cost of parking space in the inner city. Sex workers need a better location where safety, supervision, and privacy are guaranteed. We want to give neighborhoods and districts say over any placement.

125118.explanation: Housing corporations have sold off many (too many) social rental homes in recent years. The stock may not get smaller. Exceptions must always remain possible.

125119.explanation: There are already various places where materials are recycled and reused. We have environmental disposal sites and repair cafes in several locations.

125120.explanation: If multiple different road users must share the roadway, it becomes an unclear mess of traffic flows at different speeds. This creates insecurity for all users and stagnates car and bus traffic.

125122.explanation: The average Utrecht entrepreneur is already too limited by all climate plans. We stand for a cleaner environment and remain realistic in this.

125063.explanation: According to Link, there is no asylum crisis, but a shelter crisis. Link stands firmly behind the distribution law.

125066.explanation: Link wants to phase out open fireplaces as quickly as possible, but (modern) efficient wood stoves are allowed in our view. It is important to actively inform people about burning alerts.

125070.explanation: Link believes festivals and parties make a positive contribution to a city but also recognizes that a noise standard can be desirable.

125073.explanation: Link believes that good, independent journalism is indispensable in a democratic society.

125078.explanation: Link is not opposed to a financial boost for enforcement officers (boa’s). However, we believe in quality over quantity. Link therefore thinks that funds should go to training in areas such as cultural sensitivity and anti-racism for enforcement officers. Wages may also increase.

125081.explanation: Link is absolutely against an expansion of the monopoly on violence. We think enforcement officers would benefit more from training such as non-violent communication, cultural sensitivity, and anti-racism instead of batons.

125082.explanation: Link believes this will contribute to driving up house prices. This is undesirable. We find it important that more affordable housing is created and that affordable homeownership remains affordable in the long term.

125083.explanation: Link opposes priority for all Utrecht residents; however, we support neighborhood priority in certain districts to promote rootedness. For example, neighborhood priority is very desirable in Overvecht center.

125090.explanation: Link believes the income threshold should be raised and that more customized approaches are needed to consider the income, expenses, and specific situation of a household. Currently, there are still too many working poor who fall between the cracks.

125098.explanation: Link advocates that all care should be offered with sensitivity to diversity, so everyone can identify with and feel welcome in the care they need. Additionally, it can be very helpful to have specialized care tailored to specific needs.

125100.explanation: Period poverty is a persistent problem that has no place in an equitable society. Free tampons and sanitary pads are one way to combat this.

125101.explanation: If there is a necessity to increase the property tax (ozb), Link agrees that it can be raised.

125110.explanation: Link believes that 50% of all newly built homes should be social rental housing. There is currently still a significant shortage.

125115.explanation: According to Link, supply deliveries and people who rely on a car are exempt from the ban. Think of people with physical disabilities, the chronically ill, or people who depend on a car for their profession.

125118.explanation: Link believes the sale of social rental homes must immediately stop. The share of social rental housing has been declining for years, causing ever longer waiting lists.

125120.explanation: As long as there is no regulation regarding minimum age and similar factors, Link opposes moving these road users to the carriageway. Relatively young children and children in cargo bikes would then become part of traffic among cars, and Link believes safety must always come first.

statements.title: All e‑bikes, fatbikes and faster cargo bikes should be moved from the cycle path to the roadway.
Utrecht should (continue to) provide shelter for rejected asylum seekers.
Residents of Utrecht should be able to give their opinion on important subjects via a referendum.

Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.
